VEGANScreener + metabolomics

Q3: Associations between PDI and the metabolome in vegans.

This code is used to explore patterns in the metabolome profiles of vegans, particularly by linking them to the PDI score

The script is divided into two parts, according to the integration method of metabolome datasets (see preprocessing scripts for details). At first, the sPCA dataset is analyzed. This dataset is used for multivariate analysis, PCA visualization, PERMANOVA and ML approach. The concatenated dataset of both LCMS and NMR is used for univariate analysis.

The main parts of the analysis is composed of:

  1. Global visualization - 3 components color-coding PDI

  2. PERMANOVA - how much of the variation can be explained by various external variables: dist(Metabolome) ~ sex + bmi + age + veganDuration + country*PDI

  3. Univariate analysis (feature-wise model) - linear modelling of metabolite using various external variables: Metabolite ~ sex + bmi + age + veganDuration + Country*PDI

  4. Random fores (RF) classification - the model is trained and validated through bootstrapping with 500 iterations. The RF model is used to distinguish between participants with 1st and 3rd tercile of PDI. The two types of models are trained:

  1. ML model on original sPCA dataset
  2. ML model on sPCA dataset after regressing out the covariates.

1 Import

metadata_df <- read.table(
  file =  "../../data/preprocessed/metadata_preprocessed_v3.tsv",
  header = TRUE) 

lcms_df <- read.table(
  file= "../../data/preprocessed/metabolomics/lc_ms_preprocessed_INT_v3.tsv",
  check.names = FALSE)

nmr_df <- read.table(
  file = "../../data/preprocessed/metabolomics/nmr_preprocessed_INT_v3.tsv",
  check.names = FALSE) 

spca_integrated_df <-  read.table(
  file = "../../data/preprocessed/metabolomics/spca_integrated_vegans_INT_v3.tsv",
  check.names = FALSE) 

pdi_df <- read.table(
  file = "../../data/preprocessed/dietary/pdi/pdi_avg_residuals_scaled_v3.tsv",
  check.names = FALSE,
  header = TRUE) %>% 
  dplyr::select(study_id,pdi_an_avg,hpdi_an_avg,updi_an_avg) 

metadata_df <- merge(metadata_df,pdi_df,by = "study_id",all.x = TRUE)
metadata_df <- metadata_df %>% dplyr::filter(!is.na(pdi_an_avg))
nmr_df <- nmr_df %>% dplyr::filter(rownames(.) %in% metadata_df$study_id)
lcms_df <- lcms_df %>% dplyr::filter(rownames(.) %in% metadata_df$study_id)
spca_integrated_df <- spca_integrated_df %>% dplyr::filter(rownames(.) %in% metadata_df$study_id)

Scaling - when using columns from metadata as covariates, they need to be scaled, so mean is 0 and SD is 0.

metadata_scaled_df <- metadata_df
metadata_scaled_df[,-c(1,3,5,6,8)] <- apply(metadata_df[,-c(1,3,5,6,8)],2,scale)
metadata_scaled_df <- metadata_scaled_df %>%
  mutate(sex=as.factor(sex),
         country=as.factor(country),
         group=as.factor(group),
         diet_supplements=as.factor(diet_supplements))

2 Descriptive statistics

Summary of counts per diet group, country and sex:

metadata_df %>% dplyr::group_by(country,sex) %>% count()
# A tibble: 10 × 3
# Groups:   country, sex [10]
   country sex       n
   <chr>   <chr> <int>
 1 BE      F        52
 2 BE      M        27
 3 CH      F        23
 4 CH      M        20
 5 CZ      F        36
 6 CZ      M        35
 7 DE      F        50
 8 DE      M        42
 9 ES      F        28
10 ES      M        15

Basic plots for visualizing the distribution of selected variables:

plot_list <- list()
name <- c("Age (years)","BMI","Vegan diet duration (years)","VS_total","UPF [%]",
          "PDI")
i <- 1
for (col in c("age","bmi","vegan_duration","VS_total","UPF_perc_avg",
              "pdi_an_avg")){
  p <- ggplot(metadata_df) + 
    geom_histogram(aes(x=!!sym(col)),fill="darkblue") + 
    theme_minimal() + 
    theme(axis.title = element_text(face="bold",size=20),
          axis.text=element_text(face="bold",size=20),
          title = element_text(face = "plain",size=20)) + 
    xlab(name[i]) +
    ggtitle(paste(round(mean(metadata_df[,col],na.rm = TRUE),2),"+-", round(sd(metadata_df[,col],na.rm = TRUE),2)))
  
  plot_list[[length(plot_list)+1]] <- p
  i <- i + 1 
                                            
}
i <- 1
name <- c("Sex","Country","Supplementation")
for (col in c("sex","country","diet_supplements")){
  p <- ggplot(metadata_df) +
        theme_minimal() + 
     theme(axis.title = element_text(face="bold",size=20),
          axis.text=element_text(face="bold",size=20),
          axis.text.x=element_text(angle = 45,hjust = 1)) +
    stat_count(aes(x=!!sym(col)),fill="darkblue") + 
    xlab(name[i]) +
  geom_text(data=metadata_df %>% 
              dplyr::group_by(!!sym(col)) %>% count() %>%
              dplyr::mutate(n=n+10), aes(x=!!sym(col),y=n,label=n))
  
  plot_list[[length(plot_list)+1]] <- p
                 
  i <- i+1                           
}

wrap_plots(plot_list)

3 SPCA

The SPCA dataset was created by concatenating LCMS and NMR dataset and applying Mixomics::spca() function. See preprocessing script for details.

mixOmics::spca() performs Sparse Principal Component Analysis, which is similar to regular PCA but adds a sparsity constraint so that only a subset of variables contributes to each component. This sparsity is controlled by penalization, making interpretation easier because irrelevant or noisy variables get loadings of zero.

3.1 Visualization

3.1.1 PCA

spca_plot_df <- merge(
  spca_integrated_df %>% rownames_to_column("study_id"),
  metadata_df,by="study_id")

p <- pca_plot(spca_integrated_df,
              metadata_df,
              color_col = "pdi_an_avg",
              perform_pca = FALSE)

p <- ggtheme(p)

p

p_pca <- list()
p_pca[[1]] <- p
f3a <- p
save(f3a,file="../results/vegan/figures/f3a.RData")
png 
  2 
spca_plot_df <- merge(
  spca_integrated_df %>% rownames_to_column("study_id"),
  metadata_df,by="study_id")

p1 <- pca_plot(spca_integrated_df,
              metadata_df,
              color_col = "pdi_an_avg",
              perform_pca = FALSE,axis = c("PC1","PC2"))

p1 <- ggtheme(p1)

p2 <- pca_plot(spca_integrated_df,
              metadata_df,
              color_col = "pdi_an_avg",
              perform_pca = FALSE,axis = c("PC2","PC3"))

p2 <- ggtheme(p2)

p3 <- pca_plot(spca_integrated_df,
              metadata_df,
              color_col = "pdi_an_avg",
              perform_pca = FALSE,axis = c("PC1","PC3"))

p3 <- ggtheme(p3)

p1 + p2 + p3 + 
plot_layout(guides = "collect")

png 
  2

3.1.2 Boxplots

spca_for_uni <- merge(spca_integrated_df %>% rownames_to_column("study_id"),
                      metadata_scaled_df,by="study_id",all = TRUE) 

spca_for_uni <- spca_for_uni %>%
  mutate(
    tertile_cut = ntile(pdi_an_avg, 3), # assign 1, 2, or 3 (tertile)
    tertile_label = case_when(                          # label tertiles
      tertile_cut == 1 ~ "1st_PDItertile",
      tertile_cut == 2 ~ "2nd_PDItertile",
      tertile_cut == 3 ~ "3rd_PDItertile"
    )
  )

plot_list <- list()
for (col in colnames(spca_integrated_df)){
  p <- ggplot(spca_for_uni, 
              aes_string(x = "tertile_label",y = col,fill="tertile_label")) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    ggtitle(col) + 
    theme_minimal() + 
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("#D73027","orange","#1A9850")) +
    geom_signif(comparisons = 
                  combn(as.character(unique(spca_for_uni$tertile_label)),
                        m=2, simplify = FALSE),
                map_signif_level = TRUE, step_increase = 0.1,textsize = 2)
  
  p <- ggtheme(p)
  plot_list[[length(plot_list) + 1]] <- p
}


a <- wrap_plots(plot_list,guides = "collect")

a

png 
  2

3.1.3 Heatmap

metadata_df <- metadata_df %>%
  mutate(
    tertile_cut = ntile(pdi_an_avg, 3), # assign 1, 2, or 3 (tertile)
    tertile_label = case_when(                          # label tertiles
      tertile_cut == 1 ~ "1st_PDItertile",
      tertile_cut == 2 ~ "2nd_PDItertile",
      tertile_cut == 3 ~ "3rd_PDItertile"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(-tertile_cut)
PDI_colors <- c("1st_PDItertile"="#D73027",
               "2nd_PDItertile"="orange",
               "3rd_PDItertile"="#1A9850")

p_df <- spca_integrated_df %>% t()

ann_df <- metadata_df %>% dplyr::select(study_id,tertile_label) %>%
  column_to_rownames("study_id")

ann_colors <- list(
  "tertile_label"=PDI_colors
)
names(ann_colors[["tertile_label"]]) <- names(PDI_colors)

p <- pheatmap(p_df,cluster_rows = FALSE,show_colnames = FALSE,
              annotation_col = ann_df,
              annotation_colors = ann_colors,silent = TRUE)

p

png 
  2

Tertile averages

metadata_df %>%
  dplyr::select(pdi_an_avg, tertile_label) %>%
  group_by(tertile_label) %>%
  summarise(mean_PDI = mean(pdi_an_avg, na.rm = TRUE))
# A tibble: 3 × 2
  tertile_label  mean_PDI
  <chr>             <dbl>
1 1st_PDItertile     36.1
2 2nd_PDItertile     41.2
3 3rd_PDItertile     44.9

3.2 PERMANOVA

The test show how much of the variation in metabolomics is explained by: sex - age - bmi -vegan duration - Country - PDI

Variables were added sequentially (Type I SS), so each p-value and R² reflects a partial effect, after adjusting for those above. This model accounts for variation due to sex, age, vegan diet duration, BMI and country of origin. Then accounts for the main effects of PDI. Then tests whether the effect of PDI varies by country of origin (interaction).

PDI explains 0.230% of variation after adjusting for sex, age, bmi,country and duration of the diet. This result is statistically INsignificant (p=0.590).

# Create Euclidean distance matrix
dist_mat <- vegdist(spca_integrated_df, 
                    method = "euclidean")

# Test whether vegan vs omnivore differ in metabolomics
adonis_result <- adonis2(
  dist_mat ~ sex + bmi + age + vegan_duration + country*pdi_an_avg,
  data = metadata_scaled_df , 
  permutations = 999,
  by="terms")

print(adonis_result)
Permutation test for adonis under reduced model
Terms added sequentially (first to last)
Permutation: free
Number of permutations: 999

adonis2(formula = dist_mat ~ sex + bmi + age + vegan_duration + country * pdi_an_avg, data = metadata_scaled_df, permutations = 999, by = "terms")
                    Df SumOfSqs      R2       F Pr(>F)    
sex                  1    999.8 0.05552 21.6047  0.001 ***
bmi                  1    152.9 0.00849  3.3045  0.001 ***
age                  1    258.6 0.01436  5.5888  0.001 ***
vegan_duration       1     48.0 0.00267  1.0374  0.390    
country              4   1772.6 0.09844  9.5760  0.001 ***
pdi_an_avg           1     41.1 0.00228  0.8875  0.567    
country:pdi_an_avg   4    203.5 0.01130  1.0993  0.274    
Residual           314  14531.2 0.80694                   
Total              327  18007.8 1.00000                   
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
adonis_result <- adonis_result[-c(7,9),] %>% rownames_to_column("Var")
adonis_result$Var <- factor(adonis_result$Var,levels = rev(c("sex","bmi","age", "vegan_duration","country","pdi_an_avg", "Residual")))
p <- ggplot(adonis_result, aes(fill=Var, x=R2, y="Metabolome")) + 
    geom_bar(position="stack", stat="identity") + 
  ylab("") + xlab("Partial variance explained (R²)") + 
  scale_fill_manual(values=rev(c("#ffffcc","#b3cde3","#ccebc5",
                                 "#decbe4","#fed9a6","darkred",
                                 "lightgrey")),name = "Covariate")  

p <- ggtheme(p)

p

p_pca[[2]] <- p
p_pca[[3]] <- adonis_result

3.3 RF model

3.3.1 Original model

sub_metadata_df <- metadata_df %>%
dplyr::filter(
  tertile_label %in% c("1st_PDItertile","3rd_PDItertile"))

sub_spca_integrated_df <- spca_integrated_df[as.character(sub_metadata_df$study_id),]
model_name <- "PDI_tertile_vegan" 

prepared_df <- model_preparation(sub_spca_integrated_df,
                                 sub_metadata_df,
                                 predicting_col = "tertile_label")

rf_model <- make_rf_model(data=prepared_df,
                            outcome="Group",
                            N = 500,
                           file = model_name,
                            reuse=TRUE)

roc_c <- roc_curve(model_object = rf_model, 
                   group = c("low PDI","high PDI"),CI = TRUE) 

roc_c <- ggtheme(roc_c) + 
  theme(legend.position="none",title = element_text(size=8))

roc_c

png 
  2

Accuracy

# Find the "best" threshold using Youden's J Index
# This maximizes (Sensitivity + Specificity - 1)
roc_obj <- rf_model$rocobj
best_threshold_data <- coords(roc_obj, "best", 
                              ret = "threshold", 
                              best.method = "youden")
optimal_threshold <- best_threshold_data$threshold
optimal_threshold
[1] 0.499
# saving the results
save_rf_details("../results/vegan/RF_models_performance.xlsx",model_name,rf_model)
[1] "Results saved to ../results/vegan/RF_models_performance.xlsx"

3.3.2 Adjusted model

# Covariates to adjust for
covars <- c("sex","bmi", "age", "country", "vegan_duration")
integrated_df <- sub_spca_integrated_df
all(rownames(integrated_df) == sub_metadata_df$study_id)
[1] TRUE
adjusted_data <- adjust_for_covars(integrated_df, covars, sub_metadata_df)

model_name <- "adjusted_pdi_model_vegan" 

prepared_df <- model_preparation(adjusted_data,
                                 sub_metadata_df,
                                 predicting_col = "tertile_label")

rf_model <- make_rf_model(data=prepared_df,
                          outcome="Group",
                          N = 500,
                          file = model_name,
                          reuse=TRUE)

roc_c <- roc_curve(model_object = rf_model, 
                   group = c("low PDI","high PDI"),CI = TRUE) 

roc_c <- ggtheme(roc_c) + 
  theme(legend.position="none",title = element_text(size=8))

roc_c

f3b <- roc_c
save(f3b,file="../results/vegan/figures/f3b.RData")
png 
  2 
# Find the "best" threshold using Youden's J Index
# This maximizes (Sensitivity + Specificity - 1)
roc_obj <- rf_model$rocobj
best_threshold_data <- coords(roc_obj, "best", 
                              ret = "threshold", 
                              best.method = "youden")
optimal_threshold <- best_threshold_data$threshold
optimal_threshold
[1] 0.4992667
# saving the results
save_rf_details("../results/vegan/RF_models_performance.xlsx",model_name,rf_model)
[1] "Results saved to ../results/vegan/RF_models_performance.xlsx"

3.4 RF importance

3.4.1 Original model

PC8 and PC14 are the most important for RF model.

load("../intermediate_files/models/PDI_tertile_vegan/rf_model.RData")

var_imp <- as.data.frame(importance(rf_model$trained_model)) %>%
  rownames_to_column("PC") %>%
  `colnames<-`(c("PC","Importance")) 

var_imp <- var_imp %>% dplyr::mutate(PC=factor(PC,levels = PC[order(var_imp$Importance,decreasing = TRUE)])) 

ggplot(var_imp) + 
  geom_col(aes(x=PC,y=Importance))

3.4.2 Adjusted model

The most important is PC9 - inconsistent results with above.

load("../intermediate_files/models/adjusted_pdi_model_vegan/rf_model.RData")

var_imp <- as.data.frame(importance(rf_model$trained_model)) %>%
  rownames_to_column("PC") %>%
  `colnames<-`(c("PC","Importance")) 

var_imp <- var_imp %>% dplyr::mutate(PC=factor(PC,levels = PC[order(var_imp$Importance,decreasing = TRUE)])) 

ggplot(var_imp) + 
  geom_col(aes(x=PC,y=Importance))

3.4.3 Visualization

To explore the important PCs, their visualization is shown below. We will show PC8 and PC9 as these PCs were picked by original or adjusted models. However, as the performance of the model was poor, the visualization is rather reduntant.

spca_plot_df <- merge(
  spca_integrated_df %>% rownames_to_column("study_id"),
  metadata_df,by="study_id")

p <- pca_plot(spca_integrated_df,
              metadata_df,
              color_col = "pdi_an_avg",
              perform_pca = FALSE,
              axis = c("PC8","PC9"))

p <- ggtheme(p)

p

p_pca[[4]] <- p
p <- ggplot(spca_for_uni, 
             aes_string(x = "tertile_label",y = "PC9",fill="tertile_label")) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    ggtitle("PC9") + 
    theme_minimal() + 
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("#D73027","orange","#1A9850")) + 
    geom_signif(comparisons = 
                  combn(as.character(unique(spca_for_uni$tertile_label)),
                        m=2, simplify = FALSE),
                map_signif_level = TRUE, step_increase = 0.1,textsize = 2)
  
p <- ggtheme(p) 

p

png 
  2

3.5 SPCA loadings

Here, we will explore the loadings of the most important PC (PC9)

load("../results/preprocessing/spca_result_vegans_INT_v2.RData")
df <- data.frame (
  Metabolite=names(spca_result$loadings$X[,5]),
  Loading=spca_result$loadings$X[,5])

write.table(df,sep="\t","../results/vegan/Q3a_PDI/loadings_PC9.tsv")

This plot shows the loading of individual metabolites to PC9, ordered by its absolute value.

ggplot(df %>% 
         dplyr::mutate(
           Metabolite=factor(Metabolite,
                             levels = Metabolite[order(abs(Loading),decreasing = FALSE)])
           )) + 
  geom_col(aes(x=Metabolite,y=Loading)) + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=90,hjust = 1)) + 
  coord_flip()


spca_loadings <- df$Metabolite[abs(df$Loading)>=0.1]

4 Concatenated dataset

concat_integrated_df <- cbind(lcms_df,nmr_df)

4.1 Visualization

4.1.1 Boxplots

concat_for_uni <- merge(concat_integrated_df %>% rownames_to_column("study_id"),
                      metadata_scaled_df,by="study_id",all = TRUE) 

concat_for_uni <- concat_for_uni %>%
  mutate(
    tertile_cut = ntile(pdi_an_avg, 3), # assign 1, 2, or 3 (tertile)
    tertile_label = case_when(                          # label tertiles
      tertile_cut == 1 ~ "1st_PDItertile",
      tertile_cut == 2 ~ "2nd_PDItertile",
      tertile_cut == 3 ~ "3rd_PDItertile"
    )
  )

plot_list <- list()
for (col in colnames(concat_integrated_df)){
  p <- ggplot(concat_for_uni, 
              aes(x = tertile_label,
                  y = !!sym(col),
                  fill=tertile_label)) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    ggtitle(col) + 
    theme_minimal() + 
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("#D73027","orange","#1A9850")) +
    geom_signif(comparisons = 
                  combn(as.character(unique(concat_for_uni$tertile_label)),
                        m=2, simplify = FALSE),
                map_signif_level = TRUE, step_increase = 0.1,textsize = 2)
  
  p <- ggtheme(p) + 
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45,hjust = 1))
  plot_list[[length(plot_list) + 1]] <- p
}


a <- wrap_plots(plot_list,guides = "collect")

a

png 
  2

4.1.2 Heatmap

VS_colors <- c("1st_PDItertile"="#D73027",
               "2nd_PDItertile"="orange",
               "3rd_PDItertile"="#1A9850")

p_df <- concat_integrated_df %>% t()

ann_df <- metadata_df %>% dplyr::select(study_id,tertile_label) %>%
  column_to_rownames("study_id")

ann_colors <- list(
  "tertile_label"=VS_colors
)
names(ann_colors[["tertile_label"]]) <- names(VS_colors)

p <- pheatmap(p_df,cluster_rows = FALSE,show_colnames = FALSE,
              show_rownames = FALSE,
              annotation_col = ann_df,
              annotation_colors = ann_colors,silent = TRUE)

p

png 
  2

4.2 Univariate analysis

We will test the differences between diets and VS total using generalize linear model while adjusting for covariates.

4.2.1 Continuous analysis

Call glm to explore which metabolites are significantly related to VS score while adjusting for age, sex, Country and dietary group:

Metabolite ~ sex + bmi + age + eq_vegan_dur_total + Country*PDI

results_list <- lapply(colnames(concat_integrated_df), 
                       function(x) fit_glm(x,concat_for_uni,
                                           tested_variable = "pdi_an_avg"))
results_df <- do.call(rbind, results_list)

results_df$p_adjusted <- p.adjust(results_df$Pr...t..,method = "BH")

Look at significant results - Propionylcarnitine

results_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_adjusted<0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(variable=="pdi_an_avg")
          metabolite   variable  Estimate Std..Error  t.value    Pr...t..
1 Propionylcarnitine pdi_an_avg 0.3492347  0.1221492 2.859083 0.004532938
  p_adjusted
1 0.03578635
results_all <- (results_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(variable=="pdi_an_avg"))

# save 
write.table(results_all,"../results/vegan/Q3a_PDI//lm_continous_all.tsv",
            row.names = FALSE,
            quote=FALSE,
            sep="\t")

Check the interaction effect - Carnitite,3-Hydroxybutyrate

df <- results_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_adjusted<0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(str_ends(variable, ":pdi_an_avg"))

df
         metabolite             variable   Estimate Std..Error   t.value
1         Carnitine countryDE:pdi_an_avg -0.4144394  0.1499673 -2.763532
2         Carnitine countryES:pdi_an_avg -0.5371738  0.1787127 -3.005796
3 3-Hydroxybutyrate countryES:pdi_an_avg  0.5414610  0.1968387  2.750784
     Pr...t.. p_adjusted
1 0.006055444 0.04541583
2 0.002862458 0.02424023
3 0.006290277 0.04651261
pc_to_be_removed <- df$metabolite
concat_significant_df <- (results_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_adjusted<0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(variable=="pdi_an_avg"))

concat_univariate_results <- results_df

concat_significant <- (results_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_adjusted<0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(variable=="pdi_an_avg"))$metabolite

# save and show
write.table(concat_significant_df,"../results/vegan/Q3a_PDI//lm_continous.tsv",
            row.names = FALSE,
            quote=FALSE,
            sep="\t")

knitr::kable(concat_significant)
x
Propionylcarnitine

Volcano plot

vp <- volcano_plot(results_df,comparison = "pdi_an_avg")
vp

Correlation

sign <- (results_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_adjusted<0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(variable=="pdi_an_avg"))$metabolite
df_for_plot <- concat_integrated_df %>% dplyr::select(sign)
corr_object <- cor(concat_for_uni$pdi_an_avg,df_for_plot)
corrplot::corrplot(corr_object,method = "number")

Vegan duration

results_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_adjusted<0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(variable=="vegan_duration")
          metabolite       variable  Estimate Std..Error  t.value    Pr...t..
1 Indoleacrylic.acid vegan_duration 0.1739803 0.05754176 3.023547 0.002704254
  p_adjusted
1 0.02366222

4.2.2 Pairwise analysis

Metab ~ sex + bmi + age + eq_vegan_dur_total + Country + tertile_label

LM

results_list <- lapply(colnames(concat_integrated_df), 
                       function(x) fit_netglm(x,concat_for_uni,
                                           tested_variable = "tertile_label"))
results_df <- do.call(rbind, results_list)

pdi_effects <- results_df %>%
  dplyr::mutate(p_adj = p.adjust(`p.value`, method = "BH"))

concat_significant_cat <-  pdi_effects %>%
  filter(p_adj < 0.05)
results_all <- pdi_effects

# save 
write.table(results_all,"../results/vegan/Q3a_PDI//lm_emmeans_all.tsv",
            row.names = FALSE,
            quote=FALSE,
            sep="\t")

Dispersion

dispersion <- pdi_effects %>%
  group_by(metabolite) %>%
  summarise(
    range      = max(emmean) - min(emmean),      # simplest: spread between highest and lowest 
    sd         = sd(emmean),                      # standard deviation of the 5 country means
    cv         = sd(emmean) / abs(mean(emmean)),  # coefficient of variation (relative dispersion)
    min_mean   = min(emmean),
    max_mean   = max(emmean),
    min_pdi = tertile_label[which.min(emmean)],
    max_pdi = tertile_label[which.max(emmean)]
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(range))

dispersion
# A tibble: 75 × 8
   metabolite             range    sd    cv min_mean max_mean min_pdi    max_pdi
   <chr>                  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <fct>      <fct>  
 1 Phenylbutyrylglutamine 0.478 0.258 64.9   -0.300    0.178  1st_PDIte… 2nd_PD…
 2 Lysine                 0.414 0.207 12.7   -0.185    0.229  1st_PDIte… 2nd_PD…
 3 Valine                 0.377 0.203  2.47  -0.150    0.228  1st_PDIte… 2nd_PD…
 4 Asparagine             0.361 0.183  1.97  -0.0701   0.291  3rd_PDIte… 2nd_PD…
 5 PC.20.3.OH.P.18.1.     0.354 0.177  6.23  -0.212    0.142  1st_PDIte… 2nd_PD…
 6 Lactate                0.311 0.160  4.25  -0.140    0.171  2nd_PDIte… 1st_PD…
 7 Octenoylcarnitine      0.298 0.152  1.04  -0.311   -0.0131 2nd_PDIte… 1st_PD…
 8 PC.34.1.               0.292 0.147  8.00  -0.175    0.118  3rd_PDIte… 1st_PD…
 9 Tyrosine               0.287 0.148  1.09   0.0124   0.300  2nd_PDIte… 3rd_PD…
10 Ascorbate              0.283 0.142  1.31  -0.257    0.0265 1st_PDIte… 3rd_PD…
# ℹ 65 more rows
write.table(dispersion,"../results/vegan/Q3a_PDI/dispersion.tsv",sep="\t",row.names = FALSE,quote=FALSE)
results_list_pairwise <- lapply(colnames(concat_integrated_df), 
                       function(x) fit_netglm_pairwise(x,concat_for_uni,
                                           tested_variable = "tertile_label"))
results_df_pairwise <- do.call(rbind, results_list_pairwise)

pdi_effects_pairwise <- results_df_pairwise %>%
  dplyr::mutate(p_adj = p.adjust(`p.value`, method = "BH"))

pdi_significant_pairwise <-  pdi_effects_pairwise %>%
  filter(p_adj < 0.05)

4.2.2.1 Heatmap

df2 <- pdi_effects_pairwise %>%
  mutate(
    contrast = gsub("_PDItertile", "", contrast),  
    signif = p_adj < 0.05
  )

df2$Significance <- ifelse(df2$p_adj<0.001,"***",
                                       ifelse(df2$p_adj<0.01,"**",
                                              ifelse(df2$p_adj<0.05,"*","")))
p_pairwise_heatmap <- ggplot(df2, aes(x = contrast, y = Metabolite, fill = estimate)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = Significance,vjust=0.6), color = "black", size = 2) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "darkcyan", mid = "white", high = "brown", midpoint = 0) + 
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size=4),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45,face="bold",hjust = 1),
        panel.grid = element_blank()) + 
  xlab("") + ylab("")

p_pairwise_heatmap

Network construction:

4.2.2.2 Network construction

Edge list

edges <- pdi_effects %>%
  filter(p_adj < 0.05) %>%
  #filter(abs(emmean)>0.3) %>%
  dplyr::select(from = tertile_label, to = metabolite, 
         emmean = emmean) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(from=as.character(from),
         weight=abs(emmean))

edges$from <- as.character(edges$from)
edges$to   <- as.character(edges$to)

Nodes table

tertiles <- as.character(unique(edges$from))
metabs <- as.character(unique(edges$to))

nodes <- data.frame(
  name = c(tertiles, metabs),
  type = c(rep(TRUE, length(tertiles)), rep(FALSE, length(metabs))) # TRUE = tertile, FALSE = metabolite
)

Build igraph object

g <- graph_from_data_frame(d = edges, vertices = nodes, directed = FALSE)

Visualize

VS_colors <- c("1st_PDItertile"="#D73027",
               "2nd_PDItertile"="orange",
               "3rd_PDItertile"="#1A9850")

# Assign colors to nodes: countries get their color, metabolites gray
V(g)$color <- ifelse(V(g)$type,
                     VS_colors[V(g)$name],
                     "gray70")

set.seed(123)
ggraph(g, layout = "fr") +
  geom_edge_link(aes(width = abs(weight), color = emmean > 0), alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_edge_color_manual(values = c("lightblue", "lightpink"), labels = c("<0", ">0")) +
  geom_node_point(aes(shape=type,color = name), size = 6) +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(V(g)$color, V(g)$name)) +  # node colors
  geom_node_text(aes(label = name), repel = TRUE, size = 3) +
  theme_void() +
  guides(color = "none")

4.2.2.3 Top-group assignment

All together

PDI_colors <- c("1st_PDItertile"="#D73027",
               "2nd_PDItertile"="orange",
               "3rd_PDItertile"="#1A9850",
               "other"="gray70")

# Assign colors to nodes: countries get their color, metabolites gray
V(g)$color <- ifelse(V(g)$type,
                     PDI_colors[V(g)$name],
                     "gray70")

top_group <- edges %>%
  dplyr::group_by(to) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = abs(weight), n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  dplyr::select(from,to, weight) %>%
  ungroup()

top_group
# A tibble: 2 × 3
  from           to                weight
  <chr>          <chr>              <dbl>
1 3rd_PDItertile Formate            0.325
2 2nd_PDItertile Octenoylcarnitine  0.311
top_group_viz <- rbind(top_group, 
                       data.frame(
                         from=unique(edges$from),
                         to=unique(edges$from),
                         weight=NA))

membership_df <- data.frame(
  vertex = top_group_viz$to,
  cluster = top_group_viz$from) 

membership_df <- membership_df %>% arrange(match(vertex,names(V(g))))
pdi_clusters <- (membership_df %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  summarise(metabolites = paste(unique(vertex), collapse = ", ")))[1:3,]

(membership_df %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  summarise(Number_of_metabolites = length(unique(vertex))-1))[1:3,]
# A tibble: 3 × 2
  cluster        Number_of_metabolites
  <chr>                          <dbl>
1 1st_PDItertile                     0
2 2nd_PDItertile                     1
3 3rd_PDItertile                     1
pdi_clusters
# A tibble: 3 × 2
  cluster        metabolites                      
  <chr>          <chr>                            
1 1st_PDItertile 1st_PDItertile                   
2 2nd_PDItertile 2nd_PDItertile, Octenoylcarnitine
3 3rd_PDItertile 3rd_PDItertile, Formate          
V(g)$fill <- VS_colors[membership_df$cluster]
V(g)$color <- VS_colors[membership_df$cluster]
set.seed(123)
p_all <- ggraph(g, layout = "fr") +
  geom_edge_link(aes(width = abs(weight), color = emmean > 0), alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_edge_color_manual(values = c("lightblue","lightpink"),labels = c("<0", ">0")) +
  geom_node_point(aes(size=type,color = name,fill=name),shape=21,stroke=1.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(V(g)$color, V(g)$name))   + # node colors
  scale_fill_manual(values = setNames(V(g)$fill, V(g)$name)) +  # node fill
  geom_node_text(aes(label = name), repel = TRUE, size = 3) +
  theme_void() + 
  theme(legend.position = "none")


p_all

list_core <- list()

list_core[["concat_univariate"]] <- unique(concat_significant)

5 Final significant metabolites

final_significance <- list_core[["concat_univariate"]]
final_significance
[1] "Propionylcarnitine"

6 Metabolite-based PDI

# define the variables
plot_list <- list()
rhos <- rep(NA,5)

# split the datasets for cross-validation
concat_for_index <- concat_for_uni[sample(rownames(concat_for_uni)),]
cv_splits <- cv_folds(concat_for_index,folds = 5)

# define formula - significant metabolites
vars <- sign
# Wrap all variable names in backticks
vars_safe <- paste0("`", vars, "`")

# Create the formula
fml <- as.formula(paste("hpdi_an_avg ~", paste(vars_safe, collapse = " + ")))

final_df_veganindex <- data.frame()

# cross validation loop
for (i in 1:length(cv_splits)){
  # fit the model
  model <- lm(fml, data = concat_for_uni[cv_splits[[i]]$train,])
  # predict the result on validation dataset
  VG_index <- stats::predict(model,concat_for_uni[cv_splits[[i]]$val,])
  
  # store the predicted index
  sub_df <- concat_for_uni[cv_splits[[i]]$val,]
  sub_df$VGindex <- VG_index
  final_df_veganindex <- rbind(final_df_veganindex,sub_df)
  
  # calculate correlation 
  cor_test <- cor.test(sub_df$VGindex,
               sub_df$hpdi_an_avg,
               method = "spearman")
  cor_val <- cor_test$estimate
  if (cor_test$p.value >0.05) cor_val = 0

  # plot the result
  p <- ggplot(sub_df, 
              aes_string(x = "VGindex", y = "hpdi_an_avg"),
              color="blue") +
       geom_point() + 
    geom_smooth(method = "lm") + 
    theme_minimal() + 
    ggtitle(paste("Rho =",round(cor_val,2)))

  plot_list[[i]] <- p
  rhos[i] <- round(cor_val,2)
  
}
mean(rhos)
[1] 0.048
# calculate correlation 
  cor_test <- cor.test(final_df_veganindex$VGindex,
               final_df_veganindex$hpdi_an_avg,
               method = "spearman")
  cor_val <- cor_test$estimate
  if (cor_test$p.value >0.05) cor_val = 0

  # plot the result
  p <- ggplot(final_df_veganindex, 
              aes_string(x = "VGindex", y = "hpdi_an_avg"),
              color="blue") +
       geom_point() + 
    geom_smooth(method = "lm") + 
    theme_minimal() + 
      theme(panel.border = 
              element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 0),
            panel.grid = element_blank(),
            axis.ticks.x = 
              element_line(size=0.3,color = "black"),
            axis.ticks.y = 
              element_line(size=0.3,color="black"),
            axis.ticks.length = unit(4,"pt"),
            axis.text=element_text(face="bold",size=12),
            axis.title = element_text(face="bold",size=15)) + 
    ggtitle(paste("Rho =",round(cor_val,2))) + 
    xlab("Metabolite-based PDI") + 
    ylab("PDI (z-scored)")
  
p 

f <- p + ggtitle("")
save(f,file="../results/vegan/figures/FS_PDI.RData")

7 Plot for publication

7.1 Heatmap

p_pairwise_heatmap

png 
  2

7.2 Forest plot

pdi_effects <- merge(pdi_effects,dispersion[,c("metabolite","range"),all.x=TRUE,by="metabolite"])

# Define custom colours
PDI_colors <- c("1st_PDItertile"="#D73027",
               "2nd_PDItertile"="orange",
               "3rd_PDItertile"="#1A9850")

# Optional: order metabolites by mean effect size
forest_df <- pdi_effects %>%
  group_by(metabolite) %>%
  mutate(range_eff = mean(range)) %>%
  ungroup()

forest_df$metabolite <- factor(
  forest_df$metabolite,
  levels = forest_df %>% 
    arrange(range_eff) %>% 
    pull(metabolite) %>% 
    unique()
)

# Forest plot WITHOUT ANY TEXT LABELS
forest_df <- forest_df %>% mutate(sig = p_adj < 0.05)
p_forest <- ggplot(forest_df, 
                   aes(x = emmean, 
                       y = metabolite, 
                       color = tertile_label)) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lower.CL, xmax = upper.CL), height = 0.2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_point(size=2.5,aes(shape=sig)) + 
  scale_color_manual(values = PDI_colors) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(`FALSE` = 20, `TRUE` = 19),   # 1 = open, 19 = solid
                     labels = c("ns", "FDR < 0.05"),
                     name   = "Significance") + 
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 6),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) +

  labs(
    x = "Estimated mean (with 95% CI)",
    color = "PDI"
  )

p_forest

save(p_forest,file = "../results/vegan/figures/FS3.RData")
png 
  2

7.3 Network

p_all

png 
  2
Source Code
---
title: "VEGANScreener + metabolomics"
subtitle: "Q3: Associations between PDI and the metabolome in vegans."
format: 
  html:  
    embed-resources: true
    keep-md: false
    toc: true
    toc-expand: 3
    toc-depth: 6
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: true
    number-depth: 6
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    code-summary: "Open code"
    grid: 
      body-width: 1000px
      sidebar-width: 600px
      margin-width: 100px
execute: 
  warning: false
  message: false
  echo: true
editor: source
project:
  type: default
  output-dir: output
fontsize: 12 px
---

**This code is used to explore patterns in the metabolome profiles of vegans, particularly by linking them to the PDI score**


The script is divided into two parts, according to the integration method of metabolome datasets (see preprocessing scripts for details). At first, the **sPCA dataset** is analyzed. This dataset is used for multivariate analysis, PCA visualization, PERMANOVA and ML approach. The **concatenated dataset** of both LCMS and NMR is used for univariate analysis. 

The main parts of the analysis is composed of:

1.  Global visualization - 3 components color-coding **PDI**

2.  PERMANOVA - how much of the variation can be explained by various external variables:
  dist(Metabolome) \~ sex + bmi + age + veganDuration + country***PDI**

3.  Univariate analysis (feature-wise model) - linear modelling of metabolite using various external variables:
  Metabolite \~  sex + bmi + age + veganDuration + Country***PDI**
  
4.  Random fores (RF) classification - the model is trained and validated through bootstrapping with 500 iterations. The RF model is used to distinguish between participants with **1st and 3rd tercile of PDI**. The two types of models are trained:
a) ML model on original sPCA dataset
b) ML model on sPCA dataset after regressing out the covariates.

```{r,echo=FALSE}
source("custom_functions_vegans.R")
```

# Import

```{r}
metadata_df <- read.table(
  file =  "../../data/preprocessed/metadata_preprocessed_v3.tsv",
  header = TRUE) 

lcms_df <- read.table(
  file= "../../data/preprocessed/metabolomics/lc_ms_preprocessed_INT_v3.tsv",
  check.names = FALSE)

nmr_df <- read.table(
  file = "../../data/preprocessed/metabolomics/nmr_preprocessed_INT_v3.tsv",
  check.names = FALSE) 

spca_integrated_df <-  read.table(
  file = "../../data/preprocessed/metabolomics/spca_integrated_vegans_INT_v3.tsv",
  check.names = FALSE) 

pdi_df <- read.table(
  file = "../../data/preprocessed/dietary/pdi/pdi_avg_residuals_scaled_v3.tsv",
  check.names = FALSE,
  header = TRUE) %>% 
  dplyr::select(study_id,pdi_an_avg,hpdi_an_avg,updi_an_avg) 

metadata_df <- merge(metadata_df,pdi_df,by = "study_id",all.x = TRUE)
```

```{r}
metadata_df <- metadata_df %>% dplyr::filter(!is.na(pdi_an_avg))
nmr_df <- nmr_df %>% dplyr::filter(rownames(.) %in% metadata_df$study_id)
lcms_df <- lcms_df %>% dplyr::filter(rownames(.) %in% metadata_df$study_id)
spca_integrated_df <- spca_integrated_df %>% dplyr::filter(rownames(.) %in% metadata_df$study_id)
```


**Scaling** - when using columns from metadata as covariates, they need to be scaled, so mean is 0 and SD is 0.

```{r}
metadata_scaled_df <- metadata_df
metadata_scaled_df[,-c(1,3,5,6,8)] <- apply(metadata_df[,-c(1,3,5,6,8)],2,scale)
metadata_scaled_df <- metadata_scaled_df %>%
  mutate(sex=as.factor(sex),
         country=as.factor(country),
         group=as.factor(group),
         diet_supplements=as.factor(diet_supplements))
```

# Descriptive statistics

Summary of counts per diet group, country and sex:

```{r}
metadata_df %>% dplyr::group_by(country,sex) %>% count()
```

Basic plots for visualizing the distribution of selected variables:

```{r,fig.width=15,fig.height=10,warning=FALSE}
plot_list <- list()
name <- c("Age (years)","BMI","Vegan diet duration (years)","VS_total","UPF [%]",
          "PDI")
i <- 1
for (col in c("age","bmi","vegan_duration","VS_total","UPF_perc_avg",
              "pdi_an_avg")){
  p <- ggplot(metadata_df) + 
    geom_histogram(aes(x=!!sym(col)),fill="darkblue") + 
    theme_minimal() + 
    theme(axis.title = element_text(face="bold",size=20),
          axis.text=element_text(face="bold",size=20),
          title = element_text(face = "plain",size=20)) + 
    xlab(name[i]) +
    ggtitle(paste(round(mean(metadata_df[,col],na.rm = TRUE),2),"+-", round(sd(metadata_df[,col],na.rm = TRUE),2)))
  
  plot_list[[length(plot_list)+1]] <- p
  i <- i + 1 
                                            
}
i <- 1
name <- c("Sex","Country","Supplementation")
for (col in c("sex","country","diet_supplements")){
  p <- ggplot(metadata_df) +
        theme_minimal() + 
     theme(axis.title = element_text(face="bold",size=20),
          axis.text=element_text(face="bold",size=20),
          axis.text.x=element_text(angle = 45,hjust = 1)) +
    stat_count(aes(x=!!sym(col)),fill="darkblue") + 
    xlab(name[i]) +
  geom_text(data=metadata_df %>% 
              dplyr::group_by(!!sym(col)) %>% count() %>%
              dplyr::mutate(n=n+10), aes(x=!!sym(col),y=n,label=n))
  
  plot_list[[length(plot_list)+1]] <- p
                 
  i <- i+1                           
}

wrap_plots(plot_list)

```

# SPCA

The SPCA dataset was created by concatenating LCMS and NMR dataset and applying Mixomics::spca() function. See preprocessing script for details. 

mixOmics::spca() performs **Sparse Principal Component Analysis**, which is similar to  regular PCA but adds a sparsity constraint so that only a subset of variables contributes to each component.
This sparsity is controlled by penalization, making interpretation easier because irrelevant or noisy variables get loadings of zero.

## Visualization

### PCA

```{r,fig.width=4,fig.height=3}
spca_plot_df <- merge(
  spca_integrated_df %>% rownames_to_column("study_id"),
  metadata_df,by="study_id")

p <- pca_plot(spca_integrated_df,
              metadata_df,
              color_col = "pdi_an_avg",
              perform_pca = FALSE)

p <- ggtheme(p)

p

p_pca <- list()
p_pca[[1]] <- p
```

```{r}
f3a <- p
save(f3a,file="../results/vegan/figures/f3a.RData")
```

```{r,echo=FALSE}
pdf("../results/vegan/Q3a_PDI/PCA.pdf",height = 4,width = 6)
p
dev.off()
```

```{r,fig.width=15,fig.height=4}
spca_plot_df <- merge(
  spca_integrated_df %>% rownames_to_column("study_id"),
  metadata_df,by="study_id")

p1 <- pca_plot(spca_integrated_df,
              metadata_df,
              color_col = "pdi_an_avg",
              perform_pca = FALSE,axis = c("PC1","PC2"))

p1 <- ggtheme(p1)

p2 <- pca_plot(spca_integrated_df,
              metadata_df,
              color_col = "pdi_an_avg",
              perform_pca = FALSE,axis = c("PC2","PC3"))

p2 <- ggtheme(p2)

p3 <- pca_plot(spca_integrated_df,
              metadata_df,
              color_col = "pdi_an_avg",
              perform_pca = FALSE,axis = c("PC1","PC3"))

p3 <- ggtheme(p3)

p1 + p2 + p3 + 
plot_layout(guides = "collect")
```

```{r,echo=FALSE}
pdf("../results/vegan/Q3a_PDI/PCA_all.pdf",height = 4,width = 15)
p1 + p2 + p3 + 
plot_layout(guides = "collect")
dev.off()
```



### Boxplots

```{r,fig.height=15,fig.width=20}
spca_for_uni <- merge(spca_integrated_df %>% rownames_to_column("study_id"),
                      metadata_scaled_df,by="study_id",all = TRUE) 

spca_for_uni <- spca_for_uni %>%
  mutate(
    tertile_cut = ntile(pdi_an_avg, 3), # assign 1, 2, or 3 (tertile)
    tertile_label = case_when(                          # label tertiles
      tertile_cut == 1 ~ "1st_PDItertile",
      tertile_cut == 2 ~ "2nd_PDItertile",
      tertile_cut == 3 ~ "3rd_PDItertile"
    )
  )

plot_list <- list()
for (col in colnames(spca_integrated_df)){
  p <- ggplot(spca_for_uni, 
              aes_string(x = "tertile_label",y = col,fill="tertile_label")) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    ggtitle(col) + 
    theme_minimal() + 
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("#D73027","orange","#1A9850")) +
    geom_signif(comparisons = 
                  combn(as.character(unique(spca_for_uni$tertile_label)),
                        m=2, simplify = FALSE),
                map_signif_level = TRUE, step_increase = 0.1,textsize = 2)
  
  p <- ggtheme(p)
  plot_list[[length(plot_list) + 1]] <- p
}


a <- wrap_plots(plot_list,guides = "collect")

a
```

```{r,eval=TRUE,echo=FALSE}
pdf("../results/vegan/Q3a_PDI/pca_boxplots.pdf",height = 15,width = 20)
a
dev.off()
```


### Heatmap

```{r}
metadata_df <- metadata_df %>%
  mutate(
    tertile_cut = ntile(pdi_an_avg, 3), # assign 1, 2, or 3 (tertile)
    tertile_label = case_when(                          # label tertiles
      tertile_cut == 1 ~ "1st_PDItertile",
      tertile_cut == 2 ~ "2nd_PDItertile",
      tertile_cut == 3 ~ "3rd_PDItertile"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(-tertile_cut)
```

```{r}
PDI_colors <- c("1st_PDItertile"="#D73027",
               "2nd_PDItertile"="orange",
               "3rd_PDItertile"="#1A9850")

p_df <- spca_integrated_df %>% t()

ann_df <- metadata_df %>% dplyr::select(study_id,tertile_label) %>%
  column_to_rownames("study_id")

ann_colors <- list(
  "tertile_label"=PDI_colors
)
names(ann_colors[["tertile_label"]]) <- names(PDI_colors)

p <- pheatmap(p_df,cluster_rows = FALSE,show_colnames = FALSE,
              annotation_col = ann_df,
              annotation_colors = ann_colors,silent = TRUE)

p
```

```{r,eval=TRUE,echo=FALSE}
pdf("../results/vegan/Q3a_PDI/pca_heatmap.pdf",height = 10,width = 10)
p
dev.off()
```

**Tertile averages**

```{r}
metadata_df %>%
  dplyr::select(pdi_an_avg, tertile_label) %>%
  group_by(tertile_label) %>%
  summarise(mean_PDI = mean(pdi_an_avg, na.rm = TRUE))
```

## PERMANOVA

The test show how much of the variation in metabolomics is explained by: sex - age - bmi -vegan duration - Country - **PDI**

Variables were added sequentially (Type I SS), so each p-value and R² reflects a partial effect, after adjusting for those above. This model accounts for variation due to sex, age, vegan diet duration, BMI and country of origin. Then accounts for the main effects of PDI. Then tests whether the effect of PDI varies by country of origin (interaction).

----------------------

PDI explains **0.230%** of variation after adjusting for sex, age, bmi,country and duration of the diet. This result is statistically *INsignificant* (p=0.590).

```{r}
# Create Euclidean distance matrix
dist_mat <- vegdist(spca_integrated_df, 
                    method = "euclidean")

# Test whether vegan vs omnivore differ in metabolomics
adonis_result <- adonis2(
  dist_mat ~ sex + bmi + age + vegan_duration + country*pdi_an_avg,
  data = metadata_scaled_df , 
  permutations = 999,
  by="terms")

print(adonis_result)
```

```{r,fig.width=6,fig.height=2}
adonis_result <- adonis_result[-c(7,9),] %>% rownames_to_column("Var")
adonis_result$Var <- factor(adonis_result$Var,levels = rev(c("sex","bmi","age", "vegan_duration","country","pdi_an_avg", "Residual")))
p <- ggplot(adonis_result, aes(fill=Var, x=R2, y="Metabolome")) + 
    geom_bar(position="stack", stat="identity") + 
  ylab("") + xlab("Partial variance explained (R²)") + 
  scale_fill_manual(values=rev(c("#ffffcc","#b3cde3","#ccebc5",
                                 "#decbe4","#fed9a6","darkred",
                                 "lightgrey")),name = "Covariate")  

p <- ggtheme(p)

p

p_pca[[2]] <- p
p_pca[[3]] <- adonis_result
```

## RF model

### Original model

```{r}
sub_metadata_df <- metadata_df %>%
dplyr::filter(
  tertile_label %in% c("1st_PDItertile","3rd_PDItertile"))

sub_spca_integrated_df <- spca_integrated_df[as.character(sub_metadata_df$study_id),]
```

```{r,fig.height=5,fig.width=5}
model_name <- "PDI_tertile_vegan" 

prepared_df <- model_preparation(sub_spca_integrated_df,
                                 sub_metadata_df,
                                 predicting_col = "tertile_label")

rf_model <- make_rf_model(data=prepared_df,
                            outcome="Group",
                            N = 500,
                           file = model_name,
                            reuse=TRUE)

roc_c <- roc_curve(model_object = rf_model, 
                   group = c("low PDI","high PDI"),CI = TRUE) 

roc_c <- ggtheme(roc_c) + 
  theme(legend.position="none",title = element_text(size=8))

roc_c
```

```{r,echo=FALSE}
pdf("../results/vegan/Q3a_PDI/orig_rf_pdi.pdf",width = 6,height = 5)
roc_c
dev.off()
```

**Accuracy**

```{r}
# Find the "best" threshold using Youden's J Index
# This maximizes (Sensitivity + Specificity - 1)
roc_obj <- rf_model$rocobj
best_threshold_data <- coords(roc_obj, "best", 
                              ret = "threshold", 
                              best.method = "youden")
optimal_threshold <- best_threshold_data$threshold
optimal_threshold
```

```{r}
# saving the results
save_rf_details("../results/vegan/RF_models_performance.xlsx",model_name,rf_model)
```

### Adjusted model

```{r,fig.height=5,fig.width=5}
# Covariates to adjust for
covars <- c("sex","bmi", "age", "country", "vegan_duration")
integrated_df <- sub_spca_integrated_df
all(rownames(integrated_df) == sub_metadata_df$study_id)
adjusted_data <- adjust_for_covars(integrated_df, covars, sub_metadata_df)

model_name <- "adjusted_pdi_model_vegan" 

prepared_df <- model_preparation(adjusted_data,
                                 sub_metadata_df,
                                 predicting_col = "tertile_label")

rf_model <- make_rf_model(data=prepared_df,
                          outcome="Group",
                          N = 500,
                          file = model_name,
                          reuse=TRUE)

roc_c <- roc_curve(model_object = rf_model, 
                   group = c("low PDI","high PDI"),CI = TRUE) 

roc_c <- ggtheme(roc_c) + 
  theme(legend.position="none",title = element_text(size=8))

roc_c


```

```{r}
f3b <- roc_c
save(f3b,file="../results/vegan/figures/f3b.RData")
```

```{r,echo=FALSE}
pdf("../results/vegan/Q3a_PDI/adjusted_rf_PDI.pdf",width = 6,height = 5)
roc_c
dev.off()
```

```{r}
# Find the "best" threshold using Youden's J Index
# This maximizes (Sensitivity + Specificity - 1)
roc_obj <- rf_model$rocobj
best_threshold_data <- coords(roc_obj, "best", 
                              ret = "threshold", 
                              best.method = "youden")
optimal_threshold <- best_threshold_data$threshold
optimal_threshold
```

```{r}
# saving the results
save_rf_details("../results/vegan/RF_models_performance.xlsx",model_name,rf_model)
```

## RF importance

### Original model

**PC8** and **PC14** are the most important for RF model.

```{r,fig.width=10}
load("../intermediate_files/models/PDI_tertile_vegan/rf_model.RData")

var_imp <- as.data.frame(importance(rf_model$trained_model)) %>%
  rownames_to_column("PC") %>%
  `colnames<-`(c("PC","Importance")) 

var_imp <- var_imp %>% dplyr::mutate(PC=factor(PC,levels = PC[order(var_imp$Importance,decreasing = TRUE)])) 

ggplot(var_imp) + 
  geom_col(aes(x=PC,y=Importance))
```

### Adjusted model

The most important is **PC9** - inconsistent results with above. 

```{r,fig.width=10}
load("../intermediate_files/models/adjusted_pdi_model_vegan/rf_model.RData")

var_imp <- as.data.frame(importance(rf_model$trained_model)) %>%
  rownames_to_column("PC") %>%
  `colnames<-`(c("PC","Importance")) 

var_imp <- var_imp %>% dplyr::mutate(PC=factor(PC,levels = PC[order(var_imp$Importance,decreasing = TRUE)])) 

ggplot(var_imp) + 
  geom_col(aes(x=PC,y=Importance))
```

### Visualization

To explore the important PCs, their visualization is shown below. We will show **PC8** and **PC9** as these PCs were picked by original or adjusted models. However, as the performance of the  model was poor, the visualization is rather reduntant. 


```{r,fig.width=4,fig.height=3}
spca_plot_df <- merge(
  spca_integrated_df %>% rownames_to_column("study_id"),
  metadata_df,by="study_id")

p <- pca_plot(spca_integrated_df,
              metadata_df,
              color_col = "pdi_an_avg",
              perform_pca = FALSE,
              axis = c("PC8","PC9"))

p <- ggtheme(p)

p

p_pca[[4]] <- p
```

```{r}
p <- ggplot(spca_for_uni, 
             aes_string(x = "tertile_label",y = "PC9",fill="tertile_label")) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    ggtitle("PC9") + 
    theme_minimal() + 
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("#D73027","orange","#1A9850")) + 
    geom_signif(comparisons = 
                  combn(as.character(unique(spca_for_uni$tertile_label)),
                        m=2, simplify = FALSE),
                map_signif_level = TRUE, step_increase = 0.1,textsize = 2)
  
p <- ggtheme(p) 

p

```

```{r,echo=FALSE}
pdf("../results/vegan/Q3a_PDI/important_pca_boxplots.pdf",height = 4,width = 8)
p
dev.off()
```


## SPCA loadings {#SPCA_loadings}

Here, we will explore the loadings of the most important PC (**PC9**)

```{r,collapse=TRUE}
load("../results/preprocessing/spca_result_vegans_INT_v2.RData")
df <- data.frame (
  Metabolite=names(spca_result$loadings$X[,5]),
  Loading=spca_result$loadings$X[,5])

write.table(df,sep="\t","../results/vegan/Q3a_PDI/loadings_PC9.tsv")
```

This plot shows the loading of individual metabolites to **PC9**, ordered by its absolute value.

```{r,fig.height=10,fig.width=5,collapse=TRUE}
ggplot(df %>% 
         dplyr::mutate(
           Metabolite=factor(Metabolite,
                             levels = Metabolite[order(abs(Loading),decreasing = FALSE)])
           )) + 
  geom_col(aes(x=Metabolite,y=Loading)) + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=90,hjust = 1)) + 
  coord_flip()

spca_loadings <- df$Metabolite[abs(df$Loading)>=0.1]
```

# Concatenated dataset

```{r}
concat_integrated_df <- cbind(lcms_df,nmr_df)
```

## Visualization

### Boxplots

```{r,fig.height=40,fig.width=20}
concat_for_uni <- merge(concat_integrated_df %>% rownames_to_column("study_id"),
                      metadata_scaled_df,by="study_id",all = TRUE) 

concat_for_uni <- concat_for_uni %>%
  mutate(
    tertile_cut = ntile(pdi_an_avg, 3), # assign 1, 2, or 3 (tertile)
    tertile_label = case_when(                          # label tertiles
      tertile_cut == 1 ~ "1st_PDItertile",
      tertile_cut == 2 ~ "2nd_PDItertile",
      tertile_cut == 3 ~ "3rd_PDItertile"
    )
  )

plot_list <- list()
for (col in colnames(concat_integrated_df)){
  p <- ggplot(concat_for_uni, 
              aes(x = tertile_label,
                  y = !!sym(col),
                  fill=tertile_label)) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    ggtitle(col) + 
    theme_minimal() + 
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("#D73027","orange","#1A9850")) +
    geom_signif(comparisons = 
                  combn(as.character(unique(concat_for_uni$tertile_label)),
                        m=2, simplify = FALSE),
                map_signif_level = TRUE, step_increase = 0.1,textsize = 2)
  
  p <- ggtheme(p) + 
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45,hjust = 1))
  plot_list[[length(plot_list) + 1]] <- p
}


a <- wrap_plots(plot_list,guides = "collect")

a
```

```{r,eval=TRUE,echo=FALSE}
pdf("../results/vegan/Q3a_PDI/concat_boxplots.pdf",height = 35,width = 20)
a
dev.off()
```

### Heatmap

```{r}
VS_colors <- c("1st_PDItertile"="#D73027",
               "2nd_PDItertile"="orange",
               "3rd_PDItertile"="#1A9850")

p_df <- concat_integrated_df %>% t()

ann_df <- metadata_df %>% dplyr::select(study_id,tertile_label) %>%
  column_to_rownames("study_id")

ann_colors <- list(
  "tertile_label"=VS_colors
)
names(ann_colors[["tertile_label"]]) <- names(VS_colors)

p <- pheatmap(p_df,cluster_rows = FALSE,show_colnames = FALSE,
              show_rownames = FALSE,
              annotation_col = ann_df,
              annotation_colors = ann_colors,silent = TRUE)

p
```

```{r,eval=TRUE,echo=FALSE}
pdf("../results/vegan/Q3a_PDI/concat_heatmap.pdf",height = 10,width = 10)
p
dev.off()
```


## Univariate analysis

We will test the differences between diets and VS total using generalize linear model while adjusting for covariates.

### Continuous analysis

Call glm to explore which metabolites are significantly related to VS score while adjusting for age, sex, Country and dietary group:

Metabolite ~ sex + bmi + age + eq_vegan_dur_total + Country*PDI

```{r}
results_list <- lapply(colnames(concat_integrated_df), 
                       function(x) fit_glm(x,concat_for_uni,
                                           tested_variable = "pdi_an_avg"))
results_df <- do.call(rbind, results_list)

results_df$p_adjusted <- p.adjust(results_df$Pr...t..,method = "BH")
```

Look at significant results - **Propionylcarnitine**

```{r}
results_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_adjusted<0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(variable=="pdi_an_avg")

```

```{r}
results_all <- (results_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(variable=="pdi_an_avg"))

# save 
write.table(results_all,"../results/vegan/Q3a_PDI//lm_continous_all.tsv",
            row.names = FALSE,
            quote=FALSE,
            sep="\t")
```

Check the interaction effect - **Carnitite,3-Hydroxybutyrate**

```{r}
df <- results_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_adjusted<0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(str_ends(variable, ":pdi_an_avg"))

df

pc_to_be_removed <- df$metabolite
```

```{r,collapse=TRUE}
concat_significant_df <- (results_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_adjusted<0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(variable=="pdi_an_avg"))

concat_univariate_results <- results_df

concat_significant <- (results_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_adjusted<0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(variable=="pdi_an_avg"))$metabolite

# save and show
write.table(concat_significant_df,"../results/vegan/Q3a_PDI//lm_continous.tsv",
            row.names = FALSE,
            quote=FALSE,
            sep="\t")

knitr::kable(concat_significant)
```

**Volcano plot**

```{r}
vp <- volcano_plot(results_df,comparison = "pdi_an_avg")
vp
```

**Correlation**

```{r}
sign <- (results_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_adjusted<0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(variable=="pdi_an_avg"))$metabolite

```

```{r,fig.height=5,fig.width=5}
df_for_plot <- concat_integrated_df %>% dplyr::select(sign)
corr_object <- cor(concat_for_uni$pdi_an_avg,df_for_plot)
corrplot::corrplot(corr_object,method = "number")
```

**Vegan duration**

```{r}
results_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_adjusted<0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(variable=="vegan_duration")

```

### Pairwise analysis

Metab ~ sex + bmi + age + eq_vegan_dur_total + Country + tertile_label
 
**LM**

```{r}
results_list <- lapply(colnames(concat_integrated_df), 
                       function(x) fit_netglm(x,concat_for_uni,
                                           tested_variable = "tertile_label"))
results_df <- do.call(rbind, results_list)

pdi_effects <- results_df %>%
  dplyr::mutate(p_adj = p.adjust(`p.value`, method = "BH"))

concat_significant_cat <-  pdi_effects %>%
  filter(p_adj < 0.05)
```

```{r}
results_all <- pdi_effects

# save 
write.table(results_all,"../results/vegan/Q3a_PDI//lm_emmeans_all.tsv",
            row.names = FALSE,
            quote=FALSE,
            sep="\t")
```

**Dispersion**

```{r}
dispersion <- pdi_effects %>%
  group_by(metabolite) %>%
  summarise(
    range      = max(emmean) - min(emmean),      # simplest: spread between highest and lowest 
    sd         = sd(emmean),                      # standard deviation of the 5 country means
    cv         = sd(emmean) / abs(mean(emmean)),  # coefficient of variation (relative dispersion)
    min_mean   = min(emmean),
    max_mean   = max(emmean),
    min_pdi = tertile_label[which.min(emmean)],
    max_pdi = tertile_label[which.max(emmean)]
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(range))

dispersion

write.table(dispersion,"../results/vegan/Q3a_PDI/dispersion.tsv",sep="\t",row.names = FALSE,quote=FALSE)
```

```{r}
results_list_pairwise <- lapply(colnames(concat_integrated_df), 
                       function(x) fit_netglm_pairwise(x,concat_for_uni,
                                           tested_variable = "tertile_label"))
results_df_pairwise <- do.call(rbind, results_list_pairwise)

pdi_effects_pairwise <- results_df_pairwise %>%
  dplyr::mutate(p_adj = p.adjust(`p.value`, method = "BH"))

pdi_significant_pairwise <-  pdi_effects_pairwise %>%
  filter(p_adj < 0.05)
```

#### Heatmap

```{r}
df2 <- pdi_effects_pairwise %>%
  mutate(
    contrast = gsub("_PDItertile", "", contrast),  
    signif = p_adj < 0.05
  )

df2$Significance <- ifelse(df2$p_adj<0.001,"***",
                                       ifelse(df2$p_adj<0.01,"**",
                                              ifelse(df2$p_adj<0.05,"*","")))

```

```{r,fig.height=8,fig.width=5}
p_pairwise_heatmap <- ggplot(df2, aes(x = contrast, y = Metabolite, fill = estimate)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = Significance,vjust=0.6), color = "black", size = 2) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "darkcyan", mid = "white", high = "brown", midpoint = 0) + 
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size=4),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45,face="bold",hjust = 1),
        panel.grid = element_blank()) + 
  xlab("") + ylab("")

p_pairwise_heatmap
```

Network construction:

#### Network construction

**Edge list**

```{r}
edges <- pdi_effects %>%
  filter(p_adj < 0.05) %>%
  #filter(abs(emmean)>0.3) %>%
  dplyr::select(from = tertile_label, to = metabolite, 
         emmean = emmean) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(from=as.character(from),
         weight=abs(emmean))

edges$from <- as.character(edges$from)
edges$to   <- as.character(edges$to)
```

**Nodes table**

```{r}
tertiles <- as.character(unique(edges$from))
metabs <- as.character(unique(edges$to))

nodes <- data.frame(
  name = c(tertiles, metabs),
  type = c(rep(TRUE, length(tertiles)), rep(FALSE, length(metabs))) # TRUE = tertile, FALSE = metabolite
)
```

**Build igraph object**

```{r}
g <- graph_from_data_frame(d = edges, vertices = nodes, directed = FALSE)
```

**Visualize**

```{r,fig.height=6,fig.width=6}
VS_colors <- c("1st_PDItertile"="#D73027",
               "2nd_PDItertile"="orange",
               "3rd_PDItertile"="#1A9850")

# Assign colors to nodes: countries get their color, metabolites gray
V(g)$color <- ifelse(V(g)$type,
                     VS_colors[V(g)$name],
                     "gray70")

set.seed(123)
ggraph(g, layout = "fr") +
  geom_edge_link(aes(width = abs(weight), color = emmean > 0), alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_edge_color_manual(values = c("lightblue", "lightpink"), labels = c("<0", ">0")) +
  geom_node_point(aes(shape=type,color = name), size = 6) +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(V(g)$color, V(g)$name)) +  # node colors
  geom_node_text(aes(label = name), repel = TRUE, size = 3) +
  theme_void() +
  guides(color = "none")

```

#### Top-group assignment

**All together**

```{r}
PDI_colors <- c("1st_PDItertile"="#D73027",
               "2nd_PDItertile"="orange",
               "3rd_PDItertile"="#1A9850",
               "other"="gray70")

# Assign colors to nodes: countries get their color, metabolites gray
V(g)$color <- ifelse(V(g)$type,
                     PDI_colors[V(g)$name],
                     "gray70")

top_group <- edges %>%
  dplyr::group_by(to) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = abs(weight), n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  dplyr::select(from,to, weight) %>%
  ungroup()

top_group

top_group_viz <- rbind(top_group, 
                       data.frame(
                         from=unique(edges$from),
                         to=unique(edges$from),
                         weight=NA))

membership_df <- data.frame(
  vertex = top_group_viz$to,
  cluster = top_group_viz$from) 

membership_df <- membership_df %>% arrange(match(vertex,names(V(g))))

```

```{r}
pdi_clusters <- (membership_df %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  summarise(metabolites = paste(unique(vertex), collapse = ", ")))[1:3,]

(membership_df %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  summarise(Number_of_metabolites = length(unique(vertex))-1))[1:3,]

pdi_clusters
```

```{r}
V(g)$fill <- VS_colors[membership_df$cluster]
V(g)$color <- VS_colors[membership_df$cluster]
```

```{r,fig.height=6,fig.width=6}
set.seed(123)
p_all <- ggraph(g, layout = "fr") +
  geom_edge_link(aes(width = abs(weight), color = emmean > 0), alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_edge_color_manual(values = c("lightblue","lightpink"),labels = c("<0", ">0")) +
  geom_node_point(aes(size=type,color = name,fill=name),shape=21,stroke=1.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(V(g)$color, V(g)$name))   + # node colors
  scale_fill_manual(values = setNames(V(g)$fill, V(g)$name)) +  # node fill
  geom_node_text(aes(label = name), repel = TRUE, size = 3) +
  theme_void() + 
  theme(legend.position = "none")


p_all
```


```{r,fig.height=10,fig.width=15}

list_core <- list()

list_core[["concat_univariate"]] <- unique(concat_significant)

```

# Final significant metabolites

::: {.output-background style="background-color: lightgreen; padding: 10px;"}


```{r,echo=TRUE}
final_significance <- list_core[["concat_univariate"]]
final_significance
```

:::

# Metabolite-based PDI

```{r}
# define the variables
plot_list <- list()
rhos <- rep(NA,5)

# split the datasets for cross-validation
concat_for_index <- concat_for_uni[sample(rownames(concat_for_uni)),]
cv_splits <- cv_folds(concat_for_index,folds = 5)

# define formula - significant metabolites
vars <- sign
# Wrap all variable names in backticks
vars_safe <- paste0("`", vars, "`")

# Create the formula
fml <- as.formula(paste("hpdi_an_avg ~", paste(vars_safe, collapse = " + ")))

final_df_veganindex <- data.frame()

# cross validation loop
for (i in 1:length(cv_splits)){
  # fit the model
  model <- lm(fml, data = concat_for_uni[cv_splits[[i]]$train,])
  # predict the result on validation dataset
  VG_index <- stats::predict(model,concat_for_uni[cv_splits[[i]]$val,])
  
  # store the predicted index
  sub_df <- concat_for_uni[cv_splits[[i]]$val,]
  sub_df$VGindex <- VG_index
  final_df_veganindex <- rbind(final_df_veganindex,sub_df)
  
  # calculate correlation 
  cor_test <- cor.test(sub_df$VGindex,
               sub_df$hpdi_an_avg,
               method = "spearman")
  cor_val <- cor_test$estimate
  if (cor_test$p.value >0.05) cor_val = 0

  # plot the result
  p <- ggplot(sub_df, 
              aes_string(x = "VGindex", y = "hpdi_an_avg"),
              color="blue") +
       geom_point() + 
    geom_smooth(method = "lm") + 
    theme_minimal() + 
    ggtitle(paste("Rho =",round(cor_val,2)))

  plot_list[[i]] <- p
  rhos[i] <- round(cor_val,2)
  
}

```

```{r}
mean(rhos)
```

```{r}
# calculate correlation 
  cor_test <- cor.test(final_df_veganindex$VGindex,
               final_df_veganindex$hpdi_an_avg,
               method = "spearman")
  cor_val <- cor_test$estimate
  if (cor_test$p.value >0.05) cor_val = 0

  # plot the result
  p <- ggplot(final_df_veganindex, 
              aes_string(x = "VGindex", y = "hpdi_an_avg"),
              color="blue") +
       geom_point() + 
    geom_smooth(method = "lm") + 
    theme_minimal() + 
      theme(panel.border = 
              element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 0),
            panel.grid = element_blank(),
            axis.ticks.x = 
              element_line(size=0.3,color = "black"),
            axis.ticks.y = 
              element_line(size=0.3,color="black"),
            axis.ticks.length = unit(4,"pt"),
            axis.text=element_text(face="bold",size=12),
            axis.title = element_text(face="bold",size=15)) + 
    ggtitle(paste("Rho =",round(cor_val,2))) + 
    xlab("Metabolite-based PDI") + 
    ylab("PDI (z-scored)")
  
p 
```

```{r}
f <- p + ggtitle("")
save(f,file="../results/vegan/figures/FS_PDI.RData")
```

# Plot for publication

## Heatmap

```{r,fig.height=8,fig.width=5}
p_pairwise_heatmap
```

```{r,echo=FALSE}
pdf("../results/vegan/Q3a_PDI//final_significance_pairwise.pdf",height = 8,width = 5)
p_pairwise_heatmap
dev.off()
```

## Forest plot

```{r, fig.height=15,fig.width=5}
pdi_effects <- merge(pdi_effects,dispersion[,c("metabolite","range"),all.x=TRUE,by="metabolite"])

# Define custom colours
PDI_colors <- c("1st_PDItertile"="#D73027",
               "2nd_PDItertile"="orange",
               "3rd_PDItertile"="#1A9850")

# Optional: order metabolites by mean effect size
forest_df <- pdi_effects %>%
  group_by(metabolite) %>%
  mutate(range_eff = mean(range)) %>%
  ungroup()

forest_df$metabolite <- factor(
  forest_df$metabolite,
  levels = forest_df %>% 
    arrange(range_eff) %>% 
    pull(metabolite) %>% 
    unique()
)

# Forest plot WITHOUT ANY TEXT LABELS
forest_df <- forest_df %>% mutate(sig = p_adj < 0.05)
p_forest <- ggplot(forest_df, 
                   aes(x = emmean, 
                       y = metabolite, 
                       color = tertile_label)) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lower.CL, xmax = upper.CL), height = 0.2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_point(size=2.5,aes(shape=sig)) + 
  scale_color_manual(values = PDI_colors) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(`FALSE` = 20, `TRUE` = 19),   # 1 = open, 19 = solid
                     labels = c("ns", "FDR < 0.05"),
                     name   = "Significance") + 
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 6),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) +

  labs(
    x = "Estimated mean (with 95% CI)",
    color = "PDI"
  )

p_forest

```

```{r}
save(p_forest,file = "../results/vegan/figures/FS3.RData")
```

```{r,echo=FALSE}
pdf("../results/vegan/Q3a_PDI/final_significance_forest.pdf",height = 15,width = 6)
p_forest
dev.off()
```

p_all
```

pdf("../results/vegan/Q3a_PDI//final_significance_networks.pdf",height = 6,width = 11)
p_all
dev.off()

```
